perturbation.original_text: I. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION - As used herein, Confidential Information shall mean any information and data of a confidential or proprietary nature which is disclosed by Disclosing Party to Receiving Party, including but not li mited to, customer information, proprietary technical, financial, personnel, marketing, pricing, sales, and/or commercial information with respect to computer networking, data communications, computing services; development, operation, performance, cost, k now -how, business, process and marketing of computer software and other technology relating to computer networking, data communications and computing services as well as ideas, concepts, designs and inventions, computer source and object code and computer programming techniques; and all record bearing media containing or disclosing such information and techniques that are disclosed pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: I. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION - As used herein, Confidential Information shall mean information disclosed by Disclosing Party to Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: The modification removes the specific examples and characteristics of 'Confidential Information,' making the definition extremely vague. While the agreement continues to reference 'Confidential Information,' the lack of a clear definition creates uncertainty about what is actually protected. This contradicts later sections that rely on a well-defined understanding of 'Confidential Information' for enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: II. PROTECTION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION - Both parties understand and acknowledge that the Confidential Information has been developed or obtained by stated parties by in vestment of significant time, effort and exposure, and that the confidential information is a valuable, special and unique asset of stated parties which provides each with a significant competitive advantage. Therefore, stated parties agree to hold in conf idence and not to disclose the Confidential Information to any person or entity without the prior written consent of each Party. No copying: Both further agree that they will not copy or modify any Confidential Information without the prior written consent of stated parties. No contact: both further agree that they will not directly contact any listed source of Confidential Information without the prior written consent of respective parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the detailed definition of 'Confidential Information,' making it vague. This contradicts the contradicted text, which relies on a specific understanding of 'Confidential Information' by stating that it has been developed with significant time, effort, and exposure, is valuable and unique, and provides a competitive advantage. Without a clear definition, it's unclear what information these protections apply to, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: III. NON -CIRCUMVENT - The Parties, hereby irrevocably agree, an d guarantee each other they shall not, directly or indirectly interfere with, circumvent or attempt to circumvent, avoid, by -pass, or obviate each other's interest, or the interest or relationship between the Parties with other haulers, trucking companies, contractors on project, future contractors on project, carriers, prime contractors, sub - contractors, producers, sellers, buyers, brokers, dealers, , government departments, distributors, financial institutions, technology owners, developers or manufactur ers, to change, increase or avoid directly or indirectly payment of established or to be established fees, commissions, or continuance of pre - established relationship or intervene in non -contracted relationships that by -pass one of the Parties with any hau lers, trucking companies, contractors on project, future contractors on project, carriers, prime contractors, sub - contractors, producers, sellers, buyers, brokers, dealers, government departments, distributors, financial institutions, technology owners, d evelopers or manufacturers, corporations, producer, technology owner, partnership, or individual revealed or introduced by one of the Parties to one another in connection with any on -going or future transaction or project.
perturbation.changed_text: III. NON -CIRCUMVENT - The Parties agree not to circumvent each other's interests.
perturbation.explanation: This modification removes the specific examples of what constitutes 'circumvention,' making the clause extremely broad and open to interpretation. The original text lists numerous specific entities and actions; removing these creates a contradiction because the agreement implies a specific understanding of what 'circumvent' means, but no longer defines it. This change creates uncertainty in enforcement because it no longer clarifies the exact prohibited activity. Later sections mention "non-circumvent" assuming the original specific definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: IV. AGREEMENT - This mutual non -circumvent and non -disclosure agreement is for the purpose of protecting new business developed jointly by the Parties therein. The Undersigned agree that they shall not use proprietary information or trade secrets unknown to the general public belonging to the Providing Party for their own use or for any purpose, other than the purpose authorized in writing by the Providing Party to the benefit of the joint business effort of the parties. Except as otherwise expressly set forth in this Agreement, both p arties herein will hold in confidence and not disclose, reproduce, distribute, transmit, reverse engineer, decompile, disassemble, or transfer, directly or indirectly, in any form, by any means, or for any purpose, the Trade Secrets or Confidential Informa tion or any portion thereof. The Receiving Party may only disclose the Trade Secrets and Confidential Information to its attorneys, accountants and employees, to the extent such persons have a need to know such information for the purposes described in th is Agreement, and provided each such employee shall be obligated in writing to comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreement and each such attorney or accountant shall either be legally bound to comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreeme nt or so obligated in writing. Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement with regard to the Trade Secrets remain in effect for so long as such information shall remain a trade secret under applicable law. Recipient’s obligations with regard to the Conf idential Information shall remain in effect for two (2) years after the execution of this Agreement. No contract or agreement providing for any transaction involving the Parties shall be deemed to exist between the Parties unless and until a final definit ive agreement has been executed and delivered. Nothing in this Agreement shall constitute or be deemed to constitute a partnership between the parties hereto or constitute or be deemed to constitute one party as agent of the other, for any purpose whatsoev er, and neither party shall have the authority or power to bind the other, or to contract in the name of or create a liability against the other, in any way or for any purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides a specific definition of what constitutes 'circumvention' by listing examples of entities and actions. The changed text removes these specifics, resulting in a broad and undefined term. This directly contradicts the contradicted text (Section IV) which implicitly relies on a specific understanding of 'non-circumvent' established in the original text. By removing these specific examples, the changed text introduces ambiguity where the contradicted clause expects a clear definition, creating a contradiction as it becomes unclear what actions are prohibited under the 'non-circumvent' clause.

perturbation.original_text: IV. AGREEMENT - This mutual non -circumvent and non -disclosure agreement is for the purpose of protecting new business developed jointly by the Parties therein. The Undersigned agree that they shall not use proprietary information or trade secrets unknown to the general public belonging to the Providing Party for their own use or for any purpose, other than the purpose authorized in writing by the Providing Party to the benefit of the joint business effort of the parties. Except as otherwise expressly set forth in this Agreement, both p arties herein will hold in confidence and not disclose, reproduce, distribute, transmit, reverse engineer, decompile, disassemble, or transfer, directly or indirectly, in any form, by any means, or for any purpose, the Trade Secrets or Confidential Informa tion or any portion thereof. The Receiving Party may only disclose the Trade Secrets and Confidential Information to its attorneys, accountants and employees, to the extent such persons have a need to know such information for the purposes described in th is Agreement, and provided each such employee shall be obligated in writing to comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreement and each such attorney or accountant shall either be legally bound to comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreeme nt or so obligated in writing. Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement with regard to the Trade Secrets remain in effect for so long as such information shall remain a trade secret under applicable law. Recipient’s obligations with regard to the Conf idential Information shall remain in effect for two (2) years after the execution of this Agreement. No contract or agreement providing for any transaction involving the Parties shall be deemed to exist between the Parties unless and until a final definit ive agreement has been executed and delivered. Nothing in this Agreement shall constitute or be deemed to constitute a partnership between the parties hereto or constitute or be deemed to constitute one party as agent of the other, for any purpose whatsoev er, and neither party shall have the authority or power to bind the other, or to contract in the name of or create a liability against the other, in any way or for any purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: IV. AGREEMENT - This mutual agreement is for the purpose of protecting business developed jointly by the Parties therein. Except as otherwise expressly set forth in this Agreement, both parties herein will hold in confidence and not disclose Trade Secrets or Confidential Information. No contract or agreement providing for any transaction involving the Parties shall be deemed to exist between the Parties unless and until a final definitive agreement has been executed and delivered.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of clauses detailing the restrictions on use, reproduction, distribution, and transmission of trade secrets and confidential information significantly weakens the agreement. While the agreement still references 'holding in confidence,' the specific prohibitions are gone. This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement now lacks specific guidelines on how the parties are restricted in handling confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: VI. REMEDIES - The p arties herein acknowledge that in the event of a breach of this Agreement, the injured party shall be entitled to injunctive relief as a cumulative and not necessarily successive remedy and without the need to post bond. Both parties understand, acknowled ge and agree that in the event the Disclosing Party is required to bring an action to enforce the provisions of this Agreement through a Temporary Restraining Order or other remedy, the damages to Disclosing Parry for improper disclosure of the Information are irreparable, and the Disclosing Party is entitled to equitable relief, including a preliminary injunction, in addition to other monetary relief. If any term, provision, covenant, or condition of this Agreement is held by a court of competent jurisdiction or a regulatory agency having jurisdiction over the subject matter herein to be enforceable, void or invalid, the remainder of the provisions herein shall remain in full force and effect and shall in no way be affected, impaired or invalidates an d the rights and obligations of the parties of this Agreement shall be construed and enforced accordingly.
perturbation.justification: The original text contained specific restrictions on the use, reproduction, distribution, and transmission of trade secrets and confidential information. The changed text removes these specific prohibitions while still referencing 'holding in confidence'. The contradicted text, Section VI, mentions 'breach of this Agreement' and 'improper disclosure of the Information' and the injured party being entitled to remedies. By removing the explicit prohibitions in Section IV, it becomes unclear what constitutes a breach of the agreement regarding confidential information, thereby creating a contradiction because Section VI assumes specific prohibitions exist which have now been removed, making enforcement unclear. This aligns with the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: V. TRADE SECRET (a) The undersigned and/or their employees, agents, affiliates, attorneys, accountants, and advisors acknowledge that all information shared while performing duties related to the development of potential acquisition of business or any customers introduced whether in written, printed, verbal or electronic forms by eith er party, including but not limited to files, employees, clients, suppliers, names and addresses, products samples, designs and patents, copyrights, manufacturing processes, sales and marketing projects and programs are to be held up as proprietary informa tion and a trade secret of the "Disclosing Party" and as such are to be treated in a strictly confidential manner. (b) All information provided by either "Party" to the other party shall remain the sole and exclusive property of the Provider. The undersigned shall make every effort to protect and secure the information belonging to the parties while in their possession. No information belonging to the "Parties" shall be provided or disclosed in any form by the Undersigned to a third party directly or indirect ly unless the third party is bound by the terms of this Agreement. (c) The Undersigned agrees that the proprietary information including, but not limited to, financial and marketing information, product or service information and customers' name's provided by either "Party" shall not be provided to, or disclosed directly or indirectly to any third party without prior written agreement of both parties. (d) The Undersigned agrees that no copies in any form whether magnetic, printed or electronic, abstracts or photogr aphs shall be made without written permission of the Provider {defined in III b}. Upon demand by either of the Parties, the Receiving Party herein Undersigned will return all the proprietary information to the appropriate Provider.
perturbation.changed_text: V. TRADE SECRET The undersigned agrees to treat all information shared as confidential.
perturbation.explanation: This significantly simplifies the definition and obligations related to 'Trade Secrets.' By removing the explicit requirements to protect and return the information, it creates an in-text contradiction with the remedies section that assumes specific obligations related to trade secrets are enforceable. The altered text implies a general confidentiality agreement rather than a binding requirement to return all proprietary information upon demand, making the remedies section difficult to enforce.
perturbation.contradicted_text: VI. REMEDIES - The p arties herein acknowledge that in the event of a breach of this Agreement, the injured party shall be entitled to injunctive relief as a cumulative and not necessarily successive remedy and without the need to post bond. Both parties understand, acknowled ge and agree that in the event the Disclosing Party is required to bring an action to enforce the provisions of this Agreement through a Temporary Restraining Order or other remedy, the damages to Disclosing Parry for improper disclosure of the Information are irreparable, and the Disclosing Party is entitled to equitable relief, including a preliminary injunction, in addition to other monetary relief. If any term, provision, covenant, or condition of this Agreement is held by a court of competent jurisdiction or a regulatory agency having jurisdiction over the subject matter herein to be enforceable, void or invalid, the remainder of the provisions herein shall remain in full force and effect and shall in no way be affected, impaired or invalidates an d the rights and obligations of the parties of this Agreement shall be construed and enforced accordingly.
perturbation.justification: The original text details specific obligations regarding trade secrets, including the duty to protect, secure, and return proprietary information. The changed text simplifies this to a general agreement to treat information as confidential. This creates a contradiction with the 'Remedies' section, which assumes specific obligations, such as the return of proprietary information, are enforceable. The reduced obligation in the changed text weakens the basis for injunctive relief or other remedies related to trade secret breaches, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: VII. ATTORNEYS FEES - If any legal action arises to this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to recover its court costs, expenses an d reasonable attorneys' fees.
perturbation.changed_text: VII. ATTORNEYS FEES - Each party shall bear its own costs.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the clause about the prevailing party recovering attorney's fees eliminates a significant incentive for parties to pursue legal action. This creates an in-text contradiction if the agreement elsewhere implies that breaches will be vigorously pursued or that the injured party will be fully compensated for damages. It also contradicts the purpose of having a legal agreement to begin with if there is no cost shifting in the event of a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: VI. REMEDIES - The parties herein acknowledge that in the event of a breach of this Agreement, the injured party shall be entitled to relief.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the prevailing party in a legal action related to the agreement is entitled to recover court costs, expenses, and reasonable attorneys' fees. The changed text states that each party shall bear its own costs. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the injured party shall be entitled to relief in the event of a breach. The change eliminates the possibility of recovering attorneys' fees, which could be a significant part of the 'relief' the injured party is entitled to, thus creating a direct conflict. This aligns with the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction, where essential language is removed or altered in one section, creating a contradiction with obligations or constraints stated in other sections.
